results.title: ResCon Outbound Pipeline
results.description:
Seeding-ready workflows for the GPR ResCon outbound campaign.
Contains only the workflows in the 99_rescon_outbound_scheduled
dependency tree — no investigation atoms, no baselines.

Dependency tree:
  orchestrator (rescon_outbound_scheduled)
  ├── pre-call-pipeline
  │   ├── _atoms/soql-open-cases
  │   └── _atoms/contact-for-account
  ├── [batch_call] (built-in node type)
  └── post-call-pipeline
      ├── _atoms/close-case-confirmed
      └── _atoms/close-case-no-contact

For investigation/audit workflows (describe_*, investigate_*, baselines)
see ../account-gpr/.

scenarios.name: orchestrator
scenarios.description:
End-to-end ResCon outbound pipeline intended to run on a Temporal Schedule
(see ``apps/service/scripts/apply_schedules.py``). No manual trigger_input
beyond ``phone_number_id`` + ``zen_workflow_id`` — eligible contacts come
from Salesforce, so rows are NOT user-supplied.

Pipeline:
  1. subflow    → pre-call-pipeline (Salesforce eligibility pipeline) → rows
  2. transform  → shape rows into ``{contacts}`` envelope for batch_call
  3. batch_call → fan-out dials (empty-contacts guard is handled inside
                  BatchCallWorkflow — it short-circuits to a zero-result
                  envelope, so no explicit guard node is needed here)
  4. subflow    → post-call-pipeline (SF VoiceCall + Case close per row,
                  branching on call outcome: confirmed vs no-contact)
  5. response   → summary counts surfaced to the run detail page

Co-located with pre-call-pipeline so subflow resolution stays sibling-by-name
(the engine's subflow handler rejects cross-suite filesystem refs).

scenarios.name: post-call-pipeline
scenarios.description:
End-to-end post-call writeback composite. Consumes batch_call's per-row
result envelope from the orchestrator, branches per row on call outcome,
and dispatches to the correct atom:

  reservation_confirmed=true           -> _atoms/close-case-confirmed
  not answered (no_answer/busy/failed) -> _atoms/close-case-no-contact
  answered but not confirmed           -> skipped (no case closure)
    (callback_requested, declined + transferred, wrong number)

Branching uses collected_variables from the enrichment signal, not just
the binary success flag. An answered call where the owner declined or
requested a callback should NOT be closed as "RC: Confirmed".

Pipeline:
  1. transform -> normalize batch_call results: compute outcome using
                 collected_variables, split into closeable + skipped
  2. loop      -> for each closeable result, dispatch to the correct atom
  3. transform -> roll up per-row outcomes into a single summary
  4. response  -> return aggregate counts + per-row branch decisions

scenarios.name: pre-call-pipeline
scenarios.description:
End-to-end pre-call pipeline. Inputs nothing.

Pipeline:
  01 SOQL query (filters 1..5 enforced at SF)
    -> dedupe by AccountId (group each owner's cases; preserve earliest
      future check-in as primary)
    -> Phase 1 filter: keep only accounts with count(cases) == 1
    -> loop over single-date accounts
        |-- fetch contact for account
        +-- shape eligible-callable row
    -> join with primary case context
    -> response

Phase 1 single-date filter keeps only accounts that have a single open
date in the future so that we don't have to handle multiple dates.
Phase 2 will replace this with a chronological loop across an owner's
future dates.

scenarios.name: close-case-confirmed
scenarios.description:
Post-call writeback for the ANSWERED / Confirmed branch.

Mirrors the legacy post-call logging flow — the positive
(outcome=completed) path:

  1. POST /sobjects/VoiceCall  -> create VoiceCall record linked to the
     Case + Account, with all wrap-up fields (call source, disposition,
     resort, summary, transcript, detail).
  2. PATCH /sobjects/Case/{id} -> flip Case to Closed with
     Resolution_Code__c = "RC: Confirmed" and
     Case_Resolution_Detail_v2__c = "RC: Will Use".

Both calls are made via Nango Proxy with the existing salesforce
connection (sibling pattern with soql-open-cases). No conditional logic
inside this atom — the branch decision is handled one level up in
../post-call-pipeline/.

Field surface mirrors the production VoiceCall body verbatim so the
resulting SF record is equivalent to what the legacy system creates.

scenarios.name: close-case-no-contact
scenarios.description:
Post-call writeback for the NO-CONTACT / Unconfirmed branch.

Mirrors the legacy post-call logging flow — the negative
(outcome != "completed") path:

  PATCH /sobjects/Case/{id} -> flip Case to Closed with
  Resolution_Code__c = "RC: Unconfirmed" and
  Case_Resolution_Detail_v2__c = "RC: No Contact".

No VoiceCall record is created in this branch — only calls that reach
a person get a VC record. If/when we adopt the "No Contact Case
Closure & Email" enrichment (Comments + OwnerId reassignment +
agent-routed email), extend this atom; the optional inputs
``agent_notes`` and ``agent_id`` are reserved for that.

scenarios.name: contact-for-account
scenarios.description:
Fetch the first Contact with a phone number for a given AccountId.
This is the per-account lookup the agent does after the eligibility
gate passes — it gives the dialer a real phone number.

scenarios.name: soql-open-cases
scenarios.description:
Fetch open ResCon Cases via SOQL that encodes the full pre-call contract
in code.

Filters (intersect of the customer's production ``ResCon_today`` list view
AND operational guidance):

  1. Reason = 'RC: ResCon'                         — ResCon workflow only
     (standard Case picklist; UI label "Case Reason"; NOT a __c field)
  2. Status IN ('Active','Open','Re-Opened')       — excludes Closed
  3. Total_Amount_Due__c <= 50 OR NULL             — Scribe $50 gate
  4. Subject NOT LIKE '%Will Use My Week, ...%'    — excludes WUMW records
  5. Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c >= TODAY            — past dates should be
                                                     skipped because those
                                                     reservations have
                                                     already been missed.

Filters 1-4 mirror the 6-filter set on the ``ResCon_today`` saved list
view. Filter 5 is NOT on that view — past dates should be skipped but
the SF view owner hasn't added it. Enforcing it here keeps us correct
regardless of list-view drift.

Phase 1 scope: only Accounts with exactly one future open date are
callable. Accounts with multiple future dates are deferred to Phase 2
(chronological looping). That grouping happens in ``pre-call-pipeline``
(transform node, post-dedupe) — keeping the SOQL single-shot so the
eligibility numerator (total matching rows) is still auditable vs the
final dial count.

results.title: Salesforce Atoms
results.description:
Reusable per-endpoint Salesforce smoke tests. Each atom exercises one
Nango-proxied Salesforce REST endpoint with canned mock responses in
simulation mode and live execution against a sandbox connection under
``execution`` mode.

scenarios.name: auth_check
scenarios.description:
Verify a Salesforce connection is live by calling /services/data/, which
returns the list of supported API versions. Any 2xx response means the
Nango connection is authenticated.

scenarios.name: create_record
scenarios.description:
Create a Salesforce sObject via POST /sobjects/{object}. Generic over
object type; caller supplies the object API name and a field map.

scenarios.name: delete_record
scenarios.description: Delete a Salesforce sObject by Id. Returns 204 No Content on success.

scenarios.name: describe_object
scenarios.description:
Introspect a Salesforce sObject's schema — returns fields, labels, and the
``custom`` flag. Useful before building a SOQL query or field mapping, and
critical for Data Mapper / Transform Function authoring.

scenarios.name: get_record_by_id
scenarios.description:
Fetch a single Salesforce sObject by its 15- or 18-char Id. Generic over
object type (Account, Contact, Case, ...) — the caller specifies both the
object API name and the record id via trigger input.

scenarios.name: query_soql
scenarios.description:
Run an arbitrary SOQL query against Salesforce and return the raw response
(size, done, records, optional nextRecordsUrl). Most other atoms use this
as a building block.

scenarios.name: update_record
scenarios.description:
Update an existing Salesforce sObject via PATCH. Salesforce returns 204
No Content on success — the atom surfaces that status directly.

results.title: Salesforce ResCon (GPR)
results.description:
Multi-step Salesforce integration for Grand Pacific Resorts (GPR) ResCon
campaign. Scenarios are numbered so the sequence reflects the dependency
order they were authored in:

Pre-call (read pipeline):
  01_get_open_cases           — open cases for an account
  02_get_accounts             — accounts filtered by owner
  03_get_ownership_intervals  — ownership-window windows
  04_get_eligible_accounts    — eligibility composite read
  05_pre_call_all             — end-to-end pre-call roll-up

Post-call (writeback pipeline):
  06_post_call_all            — SF VoiceCall + Case close composite

Orchestrator:
  99_rescon_outbound_scheduled — pre-call → dial → post-call end-to-end

scenarios.name: 01_get_open_cases
scenarios.description:
Fetch open ResCon Cases via SOQL that encodes the full pre-call contract
in code.

Filters (intersect of the customer's production ``ResCon_today`` list view
AND operational guidance):

  1. Reason = 'RC: ResCon'                         — ResCon workflow only
     (standard Case picklist; UI label "Case Reason"; NOT a __c field)
  2. Status IN ('Active','Open','Re-Opened')       — excludes Closed
  3. Total_Amount_Due__c <= 50 OR NULL             — Scribe $50 gate
  4. Subject NOT LIKE '%Will Use My Week, ...%'    — excludes WUMW records
  5. Waitlist_Check_in_Date__c >= TODAY            — past dates should be
                                                     skipped because those
                                                     reservations have
                                                     already been missed.

Filters 1-4 mirror the 6-filter set on the ``ResCon_today`` saved list
view. Filter 5 is NOT on that view — past dates should be skipped but
the SF view owner hasn't added it. Enforcing it here keeps us correct
regardless of list-view drift.

Phase 1 scope: only Accounts with exactly one future open date are
callable. Accounts with multiple future dates are deferred to Phase 2
(chronological looping). That grouping happens in ``05_pre_call_all``
(transform node, post-dedupe) — keeping the SOQL single-shot so the
eligibility numerator (total matching rows) is still auditable vs the
final dial count.

Why SOQL instead of list-view-by-Id:
  * Self-contained — no dependency on an SF admin-owned view that can
    drift silently (the earlier ``Copy_of_ResCon_today`` view
    ``00B1W000007elrwUAA`` lacked the Status and future-date filters,
    producing 24 already-Closed rows per run).
  * Reproducible from code — Phase 2 multi-date looping is a clean
    incremental edit to this file, not a multi-team filter change.
  * Explicit contract — every filter clause is visible in the YAML.

scenarios.name: 02_get_accounts
scenarios.description:
Fetch a list of Person Account records by Id. The agent navigates here
from the Case (Scribe step 5: "Click owner's name to open Person Account")
before checking ownership intervals or making the call.

This atom validates that an AccountId from a Case actually points to a
Person Account and surfaces the identity fields downstream steps depend on.

scenarios.name: 03_get_ownership_intervals
scenarios.description:
Fetch all Ownership_Interval__c records for a given list of Person Account
Ids. This is the source of truth for owner financial state per the Scribe:
Total_Amount_Past_Due__c, Days_Past_Due__c, Delinquent__c, Contract_Status__c.

The relationship field on Ownership_Interval__c that points to the Person
Account is `Property_Owner__c` (label "Property Owner"), discovered via
describe — NOT `Account__c` which does not exist on this object.

scenarios.name: 04_get_eligible_accounts
scenarios.description:
Given a list of Person Account Ids, return the subset that is callable
per the eligibility predicate.

Predicate (production-safe default):
  eligible :=
      (interval_count > 0)                            # cannot verify zero records
      AND (SUM(Total_Amount_Past_Due__c) <= 50)       # Scribe gate
      AND (require_active_contract = false
           OR any active Contract_Status__c)          # ignore cancelled contracts

The active-contract clause is parameterized via `require_active_contract`
(default: true → strict). Callers that need a relaxed gate (e.g. the
05_pre_call_all sandbox fan-out) pass `false` at the call site. Making it an input
keeps this subflow as the single source of truth for eligibility rules —
no duplicated logic, no bespoke `overrides` mechanism.

This is the gate test in isolation — no list view, no contact lookup,
no case context. Just "given these accounts, who passes the gate?"

scenarios.name: 05_pre_call_all
scenarios.description:
End-to-end pre-call pipeline. Inputs nothing.

Pipeline:
  01 SOQL query (filters 1..5 enforced at SF)
    → dedupe by AccountId (group each owner's cases; preserve earliest
      future check-in as primary)
    → Phase 1 filter: keep only accounts with count(cases) == 1
    → loop over single-date accounts
        ├── fetch contact for account
        └── shape eligible-callable row
    → join with primary case context
    → response

What changed vs the previous version:
  * The ``flatten`` node is gone — 01's SOQL response carries flat
    dicts already (``records[*]`` keyed by field name). The list-view
    columns-envelope unwrap step is no longer needed.
  * The 04 subflow call (past-due-sum via Ownership_Interval__c + the
    active-contract gate) is gone from the production path. The
    customer's operational eligibility gate lives on Case-level fields
    (``Total_Amount_Due__c``, plus the RC:ResCon/WUMW/Closed rules),
    not per-interval summation. 01's SOQL enforces the customer's
    definition directly. 04 remains in the repo as a drift-detector —
    if Case.Total_Amount_Due__c ever stops matching
    sum(Ownership_Interval__c.Total_Amount_Past_Due__c), the 04
    subflow's audit_all is the single place to look.
  * Phase 1 single-date filter is new. In this first phase we only
    handle accounts that have a single open date in the future so
    that we don't have to handle multiple dates. Phase 2 will replace
    this with a chronological loop across an owner's future dates.

scenarios.name: 06_post_call_all
scenarios.description:
End-to-end post-call writeback composite. Consumes batch_call's per-row
result envelope from 99_rescon_outbound_scheduled, branches per row on
call outcome, and dispatches to the correct atom:

  reservation_confirmed=true           → ../_atoms/case_close_confirmed
  not answered (no_answer/busy/failed) → ../_atoms/case_close_no_contact
  answered but not confirmed           → skipped (no case closure)
    (callback_requested, declined + transferred, wrong number)

Branching uses collected_variables from the enrichment signal, not just
the binary success flag. An answered call where the owner declined or
requested a callback should NOT be closed as "RC: Confirmed".

Pipeline:
  1. transform → normalize batch_call results: compute outcome using
                 collected_variables, split into closeable + skipped
  2. loop      → for each closeable result, dispatch to the correct atom
  3. transform → roll up per-row outcomes into a single summary
  4. response  → return aggregate counts + per-row branch decisions

scenarios.name: 99_rescon_outbound_scheduled
scenarios.description:
End-to-end ResCon outbound pipeline intended to run on a Temporal Schedule
(see ``apps/service/scripts/apply_schedules.py``). No manual trigger_input
beyond ``phone_number_id`` + ``zen_workflow_id`` — eligible contacts come
from Salesforce, so rows are NOT user-supplied.

Pipeline:
  1. subflow    → 05_pre_call_all (Salesforce eligibility pipeline) → rows
  2. transform  → shape rows into ``{contacts}`` envelope for batch_call
  3. batch_call → fan-out dials (empty-contacts guard is handled inside
                  BatchCallWorkflow — it short-circuits to a zero-result
                  envelope, so no explicit guard node is needed here)
  4. subflow    → 06_post_call_all (SF VoiceCall + Case close per row,
                  branching on call outcome: confirmed vs no-contact)
  5. response   → summary counts surfaced to the run detail page

Co-located with 05_pre_call_all so subflow resolution stays sibling-by-name (the
engine's subflow handler rejects cross-suite filesystem refs).

scenarios.name: case_close_confirmed
scenarios.description:
Post-call writeback for the ANSWERED / Confirmed branch.

Mirrors the legacy post-call logging flow — the positive
(outcome=completed) path:

  1. POST /sobjects/VoiceCall  → create VoiceCall record linked to the
     Case + Account, with all wrap-up fields (call source, disposition,
     resort, summary, transcript, detail).
  2. PATCH /sobjects/Case/{id} → flip Case to Closed with
     Resolution_Code__c = "RC: Confirmed" and
     Case_Resolution_Detail_v2__c = "RC: Will Use".

Both calls are made via Nango Proxy with the existing salesforce
connection (sibling pattern with 01/02/03/04). No conditional logic
inside this atom — the branch decision is handled one level up in
../06_post_call_all/.

Field surface mirrors the production VoiceCall body verbatim so the
resulting SF record is equivalent to what the legacy system creates.

scenarios.name: case_close_no_contact
scenarios.description:
Post-call writeback for the NO-CONTACT / Unconfirmed branch.

Mirrors the legacy post-call logging flow — the negative
(outcome != "completed") path:

  PATCH /sobjects/Case/{id} → flip Case to Closed with
  Resolution_Code__c = "RC: Unconfirmed" and
  Case_Resolution_Detail_v2__c = "RC: No Contact".

No VoiceCall record is created in this branch — only calls that reach
a person get a VC record. If/when we adopt the "No Contact Case
Closure & Email" enrichment (Comments + OwnerId reassignment +
agent-routed email), extend this atom; the optional inputs
``agent_notes`` and ``agent_id`` are reserved for that.

scenarios.name: describe_object_generic
get_contact_for_account
scenarios.description:
Fetch the first Contact with a phone number for a given AccountId.
This is the per-account lookup the agent does after the eligibility
gate passes — it gives the dialer a real phone number.

scenarios.name: investigate_resort_fields
scenarios.description:
Diagnostic atom — investigates which Case field(s) carry the resort
name for the GPR ResCon workflow. The pre-call pipeline reads
``Resort_Name__c`` but it returned null for Case 500Su00000cHOPsIAO
(Manuel Mcdaniel, Carlsbad Inn Beach Resort) even though the SF UI
shows a resort value under "Related GPR Resort".

Queries multiple Cases and pulls all resort-like fields so we can see
which one is populated where.

Read-only SOQL — no records are created or modified.

scenarios.name: investigate_voicecall_pattern
scenarios.description:
Diagnostic atom — answers three open investigation questions in a
single SF round-trip:

  1. What does the existing VoiceCall record for Case 02287032 look
     like? (node 1 — detailed SOQL for every field we care about).
  2. Across recent ResCon Cases, how many VoiceCall children does
     each one have? Is the pattern "1 VC per attempt" or "1 VC only
     on answer"? (node 2 — subquery-SOQL + histogram transform).
  3. What disposition/source/detail string combinations actually
     exist in the VoiceCall corpus today? (node 3 — distinct value
     enumeration so we know which restricted-picklist values
     06_post_call_all is allowed to write back).

Answers #22 (VC count/pattern) + partial answer to #21 (VC field
inventory on a real record) without another Chrome click. No input;
runs against the seeded sandbox.

Note: read-only SOQL — no records are created or modified.

scenarios.name: sample_voicecall_content
scenarios.description:
Diagnostic atom — SOQL-queries VoiceCall records that have either
``Summary__c`` or ``Description`` populated and returns the first 5,
complete with both text fields plus enough context
(Case__c, Account__c, Call_Disposition__c, timestamps) to judge
whether the record is a real conversation or a dial-failure stub.

Purpose: the post-call atom we ship writes an AI-generated summary
back to VoiceCall, but we don't know what the humans already put in
``Summary__c`` / ``Description``. Before we template the summary we
want to see the shape — is it a transcript? A prose paragraph? A
disposition code? A few bullets?

Used by the audit to pin down target format/length. Also acts as a
drift detector — if one day these fields are empty across the sample
it means enrichment is off and the AI summary becomes the sole source
of truth.

Note: read-only SOQL — no records are created or modified.

scenarios.name: soql_query
scenarios.description:
Run any SOQL query against the sandbox. Pass the full query string as
input. Useful for ad-hoc investigation without touching the code.

Example input:
  { "q": "SELECT Id, Name, Stay_Type__c FROM SPI_Reservation__c LIMIT 5" }

results.title: Outbound Atoms
results.description:
Vendor-neutral outbound-calling primitives. Each atom exercises one piece
of the outbound pattern (pre-call prep, the child dial workflow, reduction
of per-call results) using canned mocks in simulation mode. Authors pair
these with their own connector atoms (Salesforce, HubSpot, etc.) inside a
scenario — the outbound set itself stays connector-free.

scenarios.name: batch_call_smoke
scenarios.description:
End-to-end parent DAG composing the 4 outbound templates:
pre-call transform → batch dial fan-out → post-call reduce.

Phase 2 ships this as a YAML-parses-and-validates atom only; the
``batch_call`` node cannot run outside a Temporal context, so the atom
runner skips execution until Phase 3 lands the dispatcher.

scenarios.name: outbound_call
scenarios.description:
Single-dial template that ``batch_call`` spawns as a child workflow per
contact. At runtime the Temporal activity POSTs voice-py, then the workflow
waits for a Twilio-status signal and resolves to a
``{status, duration_seconds}`` result.

In simulation this atom exercises just the ``http_proxy`` + response shape;
Phase 3 wires the signal-wait around it.

scenarios.name: post_call_reducer
scenarios.description:
Shape a final report from the per-call results ``batch_call`` returns.
Counts completed / failed / total-duration and exposes a compact summary
downstream scenarios can dump to sinks (CRM, dashboards).

scenarios.name: pre_call_pass_through
scenarios.description:
Trivial pre-call template — caller-supplied ``contacts`` flow through
unchanged. Useful as the "no-op" pre-call step when the caller already
owns a shaped contact list.

scenarios.name: pre_call_transform
scenarios.description:
Shape a list of raw contact rows into the dial-ready ``{phone, variables}``
envelope every outbound template expects. Pure engine transform — no
external connector — so this atom runs without any connection config.
